text: Kanye West has settled a civil lawsuit with a paparazzo, whom he attacked in July 2013. The photographer, Daniel Ramos, had filed the suit, claiming that West had interfered with his rights to pursue a lawful occupation. Ramos' lawyer, Gloria Allred, has stated that the case has been dismissed as it was "settled to the satisfaction of the parties", adding that "one important aspect of it was an apology by Kanye West to our client, Daniel Ramos." The pair were pictured shaking hands after West's apology. No further details of the settlement have been disclosed.

text: Kanye West has settled a lawsuit with paparazzi photographer Daniel Ramos, whom he assaulted in 2013 at Los Angeles International Airport. West pleaded no contest to a misdemeanor count of battery and received probation, anger management sessions, and community service. Ramos had filed a civil suit seeking damages, alleging that West interfered with his right to work. The case was set for trial, but Ramos filed a dismissal after reaching a settlement with West. The settlement included an apology from West to Ramos, as shown in a picture of them shaking hands. This incident is not the first time West has been accused of assault.

text: Kanye West has settled a lawsuit with a paparazzi photographer he assaulted in 2013. The photographer, Daniel Ramos, had sued West for damages and accused him of interfering with his right to pursue a lawful occupation. Ramos accepted an apology from West and the two shook hands in a photo released by Ramos' lawyer, Gloria Allred. Ramos and West had previously reached a criminal settlement in which West pleaded no contest to a misdemeanor count of battery and received two years' probation and community service. Allred stated that the case sent an important message that celebrities are not above the law and cannot physically attack someone simply because they were asked a question.

text: Blues legend B.B. King was hospitalized due to dehydration caused by his Type II diabetes. His daughter, Claudette King, has since stated that he is feeling much better and is now back at home. Last year, the musician suffered from dehydration and exhaustion after a show, which forced him to cancel the remainder of his tour.

Blues legend B.B. King was briefly hospitalized for dehydration, a result of his Type II diabetes. However, he is now feeling much better and has been discharged from the hospital. King thanked his fans for their concerns and is now resting at home with his grandchildren. No further details about his condition or where he was hospitalized have been released. King, known for his iconic Gibson guitar named Lucille, has had a successful career with numerous Grammy nominations and hits such as "The Thrill Is Gone."
